items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on The Citizens Urban Cooperative Bank Ltd., Jalandhar, Punjab
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated June 25, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹5 lakh (Rupees Five Lakh only) on The Citizens Urban Cooperative Bank Ltd., Jalandhar, Punjab (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Exposure Norms and Statutory / Other Restrictions’ and ‘Comprehensive Cyber Security Framework for Primary (Urban) Cooperative Banks (UCBs) – A Graded Approach’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on The Chikmagalur District Co-operative Central Bank Ltd., Karnataka
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated June 25, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹1 lakh (Rupees One Lakh only) on The Chikmagalur District Co-operative Central Bank Ltd., Karnataka (the bank) for contravention of provisions of Section 20 read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act). This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the BR Act. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD)

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Sri Bharathi Co-operative Urban Bank Limited, Hyderabad
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated June 25, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹1.50 lakh (Rupees One Lakh Fifty Thousand only) on Sri Bharathi Co-operative Urban Bank Limited, Hyderabad (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Loans and advances to directors, their relatives, and firms / concerns in which they are interested’ and ‘Small Value Loans – Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks (UCBs)’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of t

items.title: India’s External Debt as at the end of March 2026
items.summary: The stock of external debt at end-March 2026 as well as revised data for earlier quarters are set out in Statements I (IMF format 1 ) and II (old format) . The major developments relating to India’s external debt as at end-March 2026 are presented below. Highlights At end-March 2026, India’s external debt was placed at US$ 762.8 billion, an increase of US$ 26.3 billion over its level at end-March 2025 ( Table 1 ). The external debt to GDP ratio increased to 20.8 per cent at end-March 2026 from 19.8 per cent at end-March 2025. Valuation effect due to the appreciation of the US dollar vis-à-vis

items.summary: On a review of current and evolving liquidity conditions, it has been decided to conduct a Variable Rate Repo (VRR) auction on Tuesday, June 30, 2026, as under: Sl. No. Notified Amount (₹ crore) Tenor (day) Window Timing Date of Reversal 1 75,000 1 9:30 AM to 10:00 AM July 01, 2026 (Wednesday) 2. The operational guidelines for the auction will be same as given in Reserve Bank’s Press Release 2021-2022/1572 dated January 20, 2022. Ajit Prasad Deputy General Manager (Communications) Press Release: 2026-2027/565

items.title: Supreme Court rejects Republican challenge of Mississippi mail-in ballot rule
Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook
items.summary: The decision, seen as a win for central bank independence, sends the fight over removal back to the lower courts.

items.title: Trump says U.S. and Iran to hold fresh talks in Qatar on Tuesday following weekend clashes
items.summary: The U.S. and Iran are poised to hold fresh talks on Tuesday in Qatar's capital, President Donald Trump said via social media on Monday.

items.title: The AI boom is colliding with a new threat: Severe weather
items.summary: Heatwaves and severe weather are raising risks for AI data centers, from grid strain to higher insurance and repair costs.

items.title: Supreme Court rules Trump cannot fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook for now
items.summary: The ruling by the Supreme Court means that Lisa Cook will remain as a Federal Reserve governor as her lawsuit challenging Trump's effort to fire her proceeds.

items.title: Treasury yields little changed as investors look ahead to key jobs data
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields were little changed on Monday as investors looked to key jobs data and monitored a fragile pause in hositilies between the U.S. and Iran.

items.title: Million-plus cities lead in job quality, earnings: NSO
items.summary: In India’s bustling metropolitan areas, workers are now enjoying improved job opportunities, particularly in regular salaried positions, alongside a rise in workforce participation. Although there’s a slight increase in unemployment rates, these cities exhibit a marked transition from casual labour and agriculture towards the expanding service and communication sectors.

items.title: Hindustan Copper to seek Navratna status, eyes threefold ore output by 2029
items.summary: Hindustan Copper is aiming for Navratna status, according to outgoing CMD Sanjiv Kumar Singh. The company plans to triple ore output by 2029 and has initiated steps to achieve the prestigious designation. Strong financial performance, including a near doubling of net profit and record revenue in FY26, underpins this ambition. Singh highlighted growing copper demand from renewable energy and EVs as a key growth driver.

items.title: Godrej Consumer expands pet care brand across south India, targets Rs 500 crore biz in three years
items.summary: Godrej Consumer Products is aggressively expanding its pet food brand, Ninja, aiming for a Rs 500 crore valuation within three years. After a successful pilot in Tamil Nadu, the brand is launching in Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and Karnataka, with pan-India plans by FY28.

items.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump bid to appeal E. Jean Carroll $5 million verdict
items.summary: E. Jean Carroll has said since 2019 that President Donald Trump sexually assaulted her in a New York department store dressing room in the mid-1990s.

items.title: Volkswagen layoffs: Work council says European carmaker cannot scrap job until 2030 amid 1 lakh job cuts plan
items.summary: Volkswagen's proposal to shut four German factories and cut up to 100,000 jobs is facing opposition from employee representatives. The works council insists the employee guarantee until 2030 must be upheld. Details here.

items.title: Trump bought as much as $5 million in Axon stock before ICE sought $220 million Taser deal
items.summary: Axon Enterprise has ramped up lobbying in Congress on federal law enforcement technology as ICE pursues a potential $220 million Taser deal.

items.title: Comcast jumps 9% after announcing it will spin off NBCUniversal and Sky from cable business
items.summary: Comcast said it will separate into two publicly traded companies through a tax-free spinoff of NBCUniversal and Sky.

items.title: Yes Bank to raise up to $1.7 billion via equity, debt issue
items.summary: Yes Bank is poised to raise up to ₹160 billion through a strategic mix of equity and debt offerings. The board has approved an equity issuance of as much as ₹75 billion alongside a debt issuance reaching ₹85 billion. This approach is designed to strengthen the bank’s capital framework while minimizing dilution for current shareholders and adhering to regulatory standards.

items.title: Exits from gold ETFs last week surged to year’s highest
items.summary: WGC data show that as the precious metal slumped to near $4,000/oz, outflows were nearly $7 for every $1 investment in the funds

items.title: US stocks today: US stocks open higher as US, Iran halt attacks
items.summary: Wall ​Street's ​main indexes opened ​higher on Monday as easing tensions in ‌the ⁠Middle ⁠East lifted ​sentiment following days of hostilities ​between the U.S. and Iran.

items.title: India lifts curbs on retail sale of petrol, diesel to commercial buyers from July 1
items.summary: India has removed restrictions on commercial buyers purchasing petrol and diesel from retail outlets, effective July 1. These emergency measures, implemented in June due to Middle East tensions and a price gap, aimed to ensure equitable supply and prevent hoarding. With global energy trade stabilizing, commercial users like transport operators can now buy fuel without daily limits, restoring normal access to retail pumps.

items.title: Eswari Global Metal Industries files papers for Rs 1,100-1,300-cr IPO
items.summary: Eswari Global Metal Industries is poised for a major Initial Public Offering (IPO) with an ambitious target of raising Rs 1,100 to Rs 1,300 crore. This strategy entails both a fresh share issuance and an offer-for-sale by existing promoters. The proceeds are earmarked for expanding manufacturing capabilities, clearing debts, and addressing general corporate requirements. The company has showcased impressive financial achievements for the nine-month period concluding December 2025.

items.title: BSE announces change in licensing requirements for market data products from Jan 2027
items.summary: BSE Ltd has announced a significant transition in its management of international licensing for market data products, effective January 1, 2027. This move sees BSE taking charge from Deutsche Börse AG, which previously handled these operations since their 2013 agreement. Clients outside India, however, will experience no disruption in service, while Indian clients maintain their direct access unchanged.

items.title: Gold breaks 3-day falling streak, rises ₹1,300; silver drops ₹2,000
items.summary: Analysts attribute gold recovery to bargain buying after recent correction

items.title: Oberoi Realty bets big on NCR with Rs 6,000 crore investment in Gurugram project
items.summary: Oberoi Realty is making a significant Rs 6,000 crore investment in its inaugural NCR project, a sprawling ultra-luxury residential development in Gurugram. The company sees the region as a key growth market, potentially rivaling Mumbai. This ambitious venture, featuring seven towers and retail space, aims for a substantial revenue of Rs 16,000 crore, signaling Oberoi Realty's pan-India expansion strategy.

items.title: IIP growth reached a five-month high of 5.1% in May
items.summary: India's industrial production surged to a five-month high of 5.1% in May, driven by robust growth in electricity and manufacturing sectors. This positive trend, supported by steady domestic demand, indicates a strengthening growth path. Key sectors like motor vehicles and textiles showed significant expansion, with capital goods production leading the charge. Experts anticipate continued momentum in the coming months.

items.title: Persistent Systems shares tumble 11% after Nagarro acquisition
items.summary: The sharp decline in the stock came as investors assessed the size, valuation and integration implications of the proposed acquisition

items.title: Atom Bank sale on cusp of collapse
items.summary: Shareholders targeted £600mn valuation but process has failed to attract bids at desired price

items.title: AI could become the next competitive advantage for renewable energy companies: Report
items.summary: Artificial intelligence is poised to become a major competitive edge for renewable energy firms, according to a BCG report. The focus is shifting from expanding capacity to enhancing efficiency and value from existing assets. AI can boost worker productivity by up to 25% and improve energy yield, with a strategic approach to a few high-impact use cases proving more effective than numerous experiments.

items.title: Railway Ministry declines Defence Ministry's plea to reduce military special train size, charges
items.summary: The Railway Ministry has turned down the Defence Ministry's plea to reduce the minimum size of military special trains from 30 to 20 wagons. Citing operational constraints, Railways stated that its standard costing is based on 30 wagons, and a reduction wouldn't proportionally cut costs. The Defence Ministry had requested this change along with a waiver in freight charges, but the Railway Board deemed further relaxation operationally infeasible.

items.title: Trainer aircraft crash-lands in UP; trainee lady pilot reported to be safe: DGCA
items.summary: A Chetak Aviation Cessna 152 trainer aircraft crash-landed in a Kasganj, Uttar Pradesh field on Monday. The trainee pilot, who was flying solo from Aligarh, is reported to be safe. The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has launched an investigation into the incident. Further details are anticipated as the inquiry progresses.

items.title: Himachal Pradesh to launch start-up bus scheme with up to 50% subsidy for youth
items.summary: Himachal Pradesh is launching Phase IV of the Rajiv Gandhi Swarozgar Start-up Yojana, empowering unemployed youth to run passenger buses. The scheme offers significant subsidies for electric and diesel buses, along with monthly operational incentives for five years. Aimed at boosting public transport and cleaner mobility, it will cover 1,000 routes, prioritizing economically weaker applicants and offering fare concessions for women and students.

items.title: Himadri Speciality Chemicals bets big on EV battery materials with 100 GWh manufacturing facility
items.summary: Himadri Speciality Chemicals plans to establish India's largest battery materials manufacturing facility, expecting a revenue boost of ₹30,000 crore over five years. The 100 GWh facility will produce key components for EV batteries, alongside a 40,000 metric tonnes LFP cathode plant.

items.title: Oil prices near pre-war levels — but persistent supply risks could spark a rebound, analysts warn
items.summary: Commodity strategists warn that Iranian leverage in the Strait of Hormuz will continue to weigh on oil markets and shipping costs.

items.title: Inspired by Wimbledon and the World Cup? Here's how to play sport for free
items.summary: As the world's best players begin play at Wimbledon, how can you get into sport on a budget.

items.title: British American Tobacco to cut 9,000 jobs
items.summary: The company had said earlier this year it was planning savings to make it "more digital and AI-focused".

items.title: Used-car market enters new era: Why more Indians are choosing used cars despite rising incomes
items.summary: India's used car market is booming, projected to hit $70 billion by FY31 as buyers increasingly choose pre-owned vehicles for value and smart ownership. Consumers now seek quality and convenience, with many first-time buyers entering the market through used cars. Organized players are building trust through certification and digital platforms, offering a new-car-like experience. Faster vehicle replacement cycles will further fuel this significant market expansion.

items.title: Geopolitics jitters drag Nifty below 24,000; Rupee slips on month-end Dollar demand
items.summary: The Nifty 50 settled at 23,946.25, down 109.75 points or 0.46%, while the Sensex closed at 76,728.37, shedding 372.10 points or 0.48%

items.title: From Pathaan, Jawan to Dhurandhar, India’s love affair with cinema remains intact: Cinepolis MD
Jio BlackRock AMC launches Prism Hybrid Long-Short SIF
items.summary: The launch comes at a time when Indian equity markets have experienced heightened volatility, and wider dispersion across stocks and market capitalisations

items.title: Quote of the day by Dean Williams: "The reason for dwelling on the virtue of simple investment approaches is that complicated ones, which can’t be explained simply, may be disguising a more basic defect. They may not make any sense"
items.summary: Dean Williams' wisdom highlights a crucial investment principle: if a strategy can't be explained simply, it might hide fundamental flaws. Complex financial jargon can mask risks and flawed logic. Savvy investors prioritize understanding what they own and why, focusing on clear, fundamental approaches. Embracing simplicity often proves to be a powerful competitive edge in the market.

items.title: Bandhan Bank CFO Rajeev Mantri resigns, to leave in September
items.summary: Bandhan Bank's Chief Financial Officer, Rajeev Mantri, has resigned, citing career growth as his reason for departure. Mantri, who also served as Executive President, submitted his resignation on June 29 and will officially leave on September 25. He expressed gratitude for the support received during his tenure, highlighting his contributions to the bank's strategic transformation. The bank has yet to name a successor for the key financial role.

items.title: Omaxe to invest Rs 6,200 cr to build 19 hotels in 5 states
items.summary: Omaxe, a key player in real estate development, has announced a bold initiative involving a Rs 6,200 crore investment over five years to create 19 hotels in five states. This will encompass approximately 5 million square feet of new infrastructure located strategically in urban centers, religious hubs, and major transport routes.

items.title: Puravankara aims Rs 1,000 cr revenue from 6.4 acre housing project in Bengaluru
items.summary: Puravankara is embarking on an ambitious 6.4-acre residential project in Bengaluru's Sarjapur neighborhood, with projected earnings of Rs 1,000 crore. This project, developed through a collaborative agreement with a landowner, will offer 0.8 million sq ft of saleable space. The company aims to capitalize on the joint development agreement model, minimizing initial investment while diversifying its presence in key urban markets across India.

items.title: India bonds rise as US-Iran halt strikes; monsoon risk limits gains
items.summary: Indian bonds have surged for the fifth straight session, fueled by easing U.S.-Iran tensions and robust foreign investment. The 10-year yield has fallen to its lowest point since March. However, concerns linger about a widening monsoon deficit in the central region, which could lead to food inflation and dampen market enthusiasm amid the global improvements.

items.title: Tata Motors-Iveco revenue to be $35-40 billion in 5 years: N Chandrasekaran
items.summary: Tata Motors is projecting a remarkable revenue growth of $35-40 billion in its commercial vehicle sector over the next five years from its acquisition of Iveco Group, chairman N Chandrasekaran said. This strategic decision, expected to finalise by September, aims to enhance Tata Motors' global reach with synergistic products and cutting-edge technologies.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | US Stock Market Today | Live: US stocks open higher as US, Iran halt attacks
AI isn't the biggest regulatory worry for Indian startups, data governance is: Survey
items.summary: The study, based on a survey of 550 ecosystem participants, including 350 startups, 100 venture capital (VC) firms and 100 incubators, shows that 44% of respondents identified data governance and digital trust regulations as their primary regulatory concern.

items.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures jump as investors buy the tech dip; oil near 4-month low
items.summary: US stock futures rose slightly on 29 June as investors returned to technology stocks after recent losses. The S&P 500 futures increased by 0.08%, while Nasdaq 100 futures gained 1.1%.

items.title: Oil cools, monsoon heats up concerns for India's rural economy
items.summary: The recent drop in crude oil prices is providing some relief from inflation worries. Yet, the unpredictable monsoon season poses a serious risk to the purchasing power in rural India. In light of this, businesses are reshaping their portfolios, recognizing that erratic rainfall can hinder agricultural profits and consumer spending. Optimism exists for a bounce-back if conditions change, but looming weather uncertainties threaten to disrupt corporate advancement.

items.title: TVS Motor looking ahead with 'measured optimism': CMD Sudarshan Venu
items.summary: TVS Motor Company anticipates a complex yet exciting future, aiming to meet market expectations in India despite potential weather disruptions. The company achieved a record year in FY25-26, selling 5.89 million units and becoming the world's third-largest two-wheeler maker. With a strong focus on R&D and generative AI, TVS is investing heavily in electrification and connected technologies, solidifying its leadership in the growing electric two-wheeler segment.

items.title: Expert view: See compelling setup for Indian stocks to outperform, says Bajaj Finserv Asset Management's head of equity
items.summary: Expert view: Bajaj Finserv's head of equity emphasises why Indian stocks are set to outperform other emerging markets. With improving foreign investor sentiment and attractive valuations, now may be the time for disciplined, long-term investment in equities.

items.title: Ford rehires human engineers after AI fails to match quality checks
items.summary: The car-maker found AI quality checks failed to match the skill of veteran technicians.

items.title: Govt plans Rs 5,000 crore scheme in 3 months to promote green technologies in steel sector
items.summary: The Indian government is set to launch a Rs 5,000 crore scheme within three months to boost clean technologies in steelmaking, aiming to slash carbon emissions. Titled the National Strategy for Sustainable Secondary Steel, the initiative will benefit all steel producers, with a significant portion allocated to smaller players. This move aligns with India's net-zero commitments, addressing the steel sector's substantial contribution to greenhouse gas emissions.

items.title: Industrial output grows 5.1% in May under new IIP series with 2022-23 base
items.summary: Growth was led by a robust expansion in manufacturing and supported by moderate growth in mining and electricity, according to provisional data released by the statistics ministry on Monday.

items.title: Delhi EV Policy 2.0 offers subsidies of up to Rs 50,000 on electric two- and three-wheelers
items.summary: A groundbreaking EV policy is set to roll out in Delhi, promising generous incentives of up to Rs 50,000 for electric two and three-wheelers and Rs 100,000 for dismantling old cars. With a robust budget allocation of Rs 15,000 crore, the goal is to achieve 95% electric vehicle registration by 2027, effectively phasing out petrol and CNG variants.

items.title: Aurobindo Pharma among 9 mid-cap stocks to hit 52-week highs & rally up to 20% in a month
items.summary: The Sensex fell 372 points on Monday, but nine BSE Midcap stocks touched fresh 52-week highs, signalling resilient investor confidence. Bank of Maharashtra, Thermax, Bharat Forge, Oracle Financial Services and Laurus Labs were among the key outperformers despite broader market weakness.

items.title: BPCL to buy 40% stake in Shell, Tiki Tar venture to expand value-added bitumen business
items.summary: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd. is acquiring a 40% stake in Tiki Tar and Shell India for Rs 85 crore, marking its significant entry into India's booming value-added bitumen market. This strategic move aims to capitalize on the nation's infrastructure development, particularly road construction. The joint venture will leverage Shell's technology and Tiki Tar's manufacturing capabilities, combined with BPCL's extensive distribution network, to supply advanced bitumen products for major projects.

items.title: Broker’s call: Aptus Value (Buy)
items.summary: Despite seasonality, GS3/1+ DPD are expected to edge up 13–15bps/40–45bps Q-o-Q to 1.65/6.65%; Credit costs are expected to remain firmly within the guided band of 0.5% (plus or minus 10bps

items.title: Assam Agriculture Minister Pijush Hazarika visits Tezpur litchi farmers, discusses export growth
items.summary: Prepare at least 2 lakh saplings of famous Tezpur litchi for distribution next year- Assam’s Agriculture Minister Pijush Hazarika

items.title: Rail-focused logistics firm Pristine Logistics confidentially files IPO papers with Sebi
items.summary: Pristine Logistics and Infraprojects has confidentially filed its DRHP with Sebi for an IPO. The rail-focused multimodal logistics company is expanding terminals, warehousing and cargo operations while strengthening growth with a Rs 3,422 crore coal mining contract and broader infrastructure footprint.

items.title: GIP-backed Pristine Logistics takes confidential route to IPO; picks Axis, CLSA, SBI as book managers
items.summary: The proposed listing of Pristine fits the pattern of infrastructure asset monetization in India, where a record ₹12.2 trillion government capital expenditure budget is drawing attention from global private equity and infrastructure funds, including GIP, Actis and Macquarie

items.title: PayU swings to Ebitda profit in FY26 as payments, credit businesses rally
items.summary: Backed by higher-margin products and ecosystem lending, the Prosus-owned fintech firm swung to an $18-million full-year Ebitda profit while exiting lower-value UPI categories.

items.title: Broker’s call: Tata Capital (Neutral)
items.summary: We believe TCL is well-positioned to sustain a healthy AUM CAGR of around 23% over FY26-28E, along with a calibrated shift toward higher-yielding segments and continued investments in digital capabilities

items.title: Technology to be central to Hindustan Zinc's next phase of growth: Chairperson
items.summary: Hindustan Zinc is pivoting towards becoming a future-ready energy transition company, Chairperson Priya Agarwal Hebbar announced. The firm is significantly investing in technology like AI and automation to boost productivity and safety. Diversifying beyond zinc, the company is exploring critical minerals such as lithium and rare earth elements, essential for the growing electric vehicle market and India's industrial expansion. This strategic shift, dubbed Hindustan Zinc 2.

items.title: Hindustan Zinc eyes three new metals in five years
items.summary: Hindustan Zinc's long-term strategy is to diversify beyond its existing portfolio of zinc, lead, silver and cadmium by entering businesses such as rare earths, potash and tungsten.

items.title: Putin’s fuel shortage admission signals growing strain on Russia’s energy infrastructure
items.summary: Putin's comments mark the first time he has detailed the extent to which Ukraine's deep-strike successes have hampered Russia's fuel production.

items.title: ₹34,102 crore Yamuna Water Project MoA signed by Rajasthan, Haryana for water infrastructure
Axis Bank CFO Puneet Sharma resigns to pursue new opportunities
items.summary: Axis Bank's Chief Financial Officer, Puneet Sharma, has resigned to pursue new professional opportunities. Sharma, who has been with the bank since March 2020, will step down on August 31. He previously held significant roles at Tata Capital and Citibank. The bank expressed gratitude for his contributions and wished him well for the future.

items.title: Bajaj Auto buyback opens July 1; shareholders can tender shares till July 7
items.summary: Bajaj Auto's Rs 5,633 crore share buyback will open for tender on July 1 and close on July 7. Eligible shareholders holding shares as of the June 24 record date can participate through the tender offer. The company said the buyback will not adversely impact its financial position.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex falls 372 pts to settle at 76,728, Nifty closes below 24,000 as geopolitical tensions weigh
items.summary: Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex settled 372.10 pts or 0.48% lower at 76,728.37, and Nifty 50 depreciated 109.75 pts or 0.46% to 23,946.25.

items.title: BAT layoffs: British American Tobacco to cut 5,500 jobs; outsource 3,500 in major global restructuring
items.summary: BAT is laying off 5,500 employees and outsourcing 3,500 roles as part of a restructuring effort aimed at achieving significant cost savings by 2028, while transitioning towards smoke-free nicotine products in response to shifting market demands.

items.title: Comcast to split cable business from media through NBCUniversal, Sky spinoff
items.summary: Comcast will split into two publicly traded entities, dividing its broadband operations from its media business. Details here.

items.title: Rupee slips as tepid stocks dull boost from firmer Asian peers
items.summary: The Indian rupee edged lower against the dollar on Monday, despite positive sentiment from a US-Iran de-escalation and gains in Asian currencies. A dip in local stock markets, with the Nifty 50 falling 0.4%, overshadowed these boosts. Increased dollar demand from foreign banks also pressured the rupee. Investors are closely watching US economic data, particularly the upcoming jobs report, for clues on potential Federal Reserve rate hikes later this year.

items.title: Rupee falls 9 paise to close at 94.54 against US dollar
items.summary: At the interbank foreign exchange market, the rupee opened at 94.36, then gained momentum and touched an intraday high of 94.25 and a low of 94.56 against the American currency

items.title: The AI boom won't burst all at once. It will pop in 'rolling bubbles': Macquarie
items.summary: Macquarie expects the AI investment boom to unwind through a series of "rolling bubbles" rather than a single crash, as different segments of the AI ecosystem heat up and cool down over time. Global AI investment has surged to about $850 billion in 2026, far exceeding historical technology booms.

items.title: India's industrial output quickens to 5.1% in May from 4.9% in April as manufacturing, electricity drive growth
items.summary: India's industrial production surged by 5.1% in May 2026, largely propelled by strong manufacturing activity despite a decrease in mining operations. This data, which utilizes a new base year of 2022-23 and refined measurement approaches, paints a clearer and more accurate picture of the country's industrial progress. The revised index also factors in emerging sectors, providing essential economic insights for decision-makers and businesses alike.

items.title: SpaceX's $25 billion bond sale drives huge demand - and a potential headache for investors
items.summary: SpaceX’s $25 billion debt sale drew heavy demand, but analysts warn of capital spending, refinancing and investor concentration risks.

items.title: Turtlemint Fintech shares end 11% below ₹152-IPO price
items.summary: Stock debuts at ₹134.90 on NSE and ₹136.20 on BSE against issue price of ₹152

items.title: Bajaj Auto's ₹5,632.8-cr share buyback to start from July 1
items.summary: Bajaj Auto is undertaking a buyback of up to 46.94 lakh fully paid-up equity shares of face value of ₹10 each at a price of ₹12,000 per share

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on June 29: Persistent Systems, Netweb Tech, Astral, MRF, Berger Paints, OLA among top losers
items.summary: Indian equities continued their decline on June 29, with the Nifty 50 dropping 0.46% and the BSE Sensex falling 0.44% amid renewed Middle East tensions. While pharmaceutical and metal stocks gained, auto and technology shares suffered notable losses, reflecting mixed sectoral performance.

items.title: Microfinance institutions' portfolio quality up in May; West Bengal, MP, Rajasthan laggards: Report
items.summary: Microfinance asset quality saw a positive shift in May, with loan defaults declining nationwide. However, West Bengal, Rajasthan, and Madhya Pradesh continue to exhibit higher stress levels. Despite a slight dip in active accounts, the overall loan portfolio expanded, indicating sustained borrower demand and lender prudence. This improvement suggests a healthier financial landscape for many, though regional disparities persist.

items.title: Apple accuses CCI investigators of 'copy-pasting' rivals' claims in antitrust investigation
items.summary: Apple has accused Indian antitrust investigators of merely copying rivals' claims without independent analysis in its competition law probe. The tech giant argues the findings are flawed and calls for them to be quashed, citing a lack of opportunity to present its case. This marks a significant escalation in Apple's dispute with the Competition Commission of India over its app store practices.

items.title: Stock market today: Sensex falls 400 points, Nifty 50 ends below 23,950; Reliance, Kotak Mahindra Bank among top drags
items.summary: The Sensex ended the day 372 points, or 0.48%, lower at 76,728.37, while the Nifty 50 settled at 23,946.25, down 110 points, or 0.46%. The Nifty Midcap 100 index dropped 0.37%, while the Smallcap 100 index declined 0.62%.

items.title: China's economy picks up in June on rebounding U.S. exports, analysts say
items.summary: China's economy is showing signs of picking up after a sluggish few months — thanks in part to a rebound in shipments to the U.S.

items.title: Small-cap stock under ₹50 jumps after announcement of amalgamation move
items.summary: VMS TMT's stock rose following the announcement of an approved merger with Aditya Ultra Steel Limited, aimed at creating a larger and more efficient steel manufacturing entity, pending necessary approvals. Shareholders of AUSL will receive 75 VMS TMT shares for every 100 shares held.

items.title: Blue Star MD flags 2% margin drop this summer but bets big on data centres for long-term growth
items.summary: Blue Star experienced a robust summer AC sales season, but rising input costs significantly impacted profit margins. The company's MD anticipates continued margin pressure, revising full-year guidance downwards. While the festive season offers a glimmer of hope for recovery, Blue Star is strategically investing in the high-growth data centre cooling sector, aiming for substantial revenue contribution by FY29.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz crisis: India’s decade-long energy strategy cushions supply shocks
items.summary: Domestic production gains, robust infrastructure and proactive supply management helped India navigate the energy shock without affecting households.

items.title: Hexaware Technologies stock gains 9% after expanding AI partnership with Anthropic
items.summary: Hexaware Technologies shares rose 9% to ₹539 after becoming an Anthropic Authorized Reseller for Amazon Bedrock, allowing direct integration of Claude models for enterprise use, enhancing its AI-first strategy and offering clients customized AI solutions.

items.title: ESDS eyes debut as India’s first listed data centre operator with ₹720-crore IPO
items.summary: The Nashik-based company is likely to have increased the size of its fresh issue to ₹720 crore from an initial target of ₹600 crore. The potential public listing of ESDS comes weeks after Sify Infinit Spaces decided to put its planned ₹3,700-crore data centre IPO on hold amid weak market conditions.

items.title: INERA Crop Science, CropNXT partner to tap Rs 500 crore biological agri-input market
items.summary: In a strategic move, INERA Crop Science has teamed up with CropNXT Solutions to tap into a Rs 500 crore opportunity in biological agri-inputs across northern and eastern India. This collaboration aspires to assist over 65 lakh farmers in five states by providing sustainable substitutes for traditional fertilizers, addressing concerns over soil health and soaring prices.

items.title: Delhi EV policy gets Cabinet nod, govt to invest Rs 15,000 crore to boost green mobility
items.summary: Delhi's Cabinet has greenlit an electric vehicle policy, set to launch by July 1, with a substantial Rs 15,000 crore investment planned over four years. The policy may offer a 50% road tax waiver on hybrid vehicles up to Rs 30 lakh, aiming to accelerate electric mobility adoption despite some internal debate. This move signals a significant push towards greener transportation in the capital.

items.title: Economic indicators point to resilient Indian economy despite global headwinds: MoSPI Secretary
items.summary: India's economy is demonstrating remarkable resilience against global challenges, according to Saurabh Garg, Secretary of MoSPI. He highlighted that initial economic indicators suggest continued growth, with the government actively leveraging administrative data for informed policymaking. The ministry is focused on harmonizing data to enhance interoperability and accessibility, ensuring a robust data ecosystem for future decision-making and the nation's development goals.

items.title: Goldman backs India 30-year bonds as Iran war impact contained
items.summary: Goldman Sachs has recommended buying India's 30-year government bonds, citing easing inflation expectations, lower oil prices and reduced fiscal risks after the US-Iran ceasefire. The brokerage believes the long end of the bond curve offers better value as shorter-term bonds have already rallied.

items.title: Housing sales fall 6% across top 7 cities in Q2 as West Asia conflict dents buyer sentiment; launches rise 7%: ANAROCK
items.summary: Persistent Systems' shares plunged nearly 11% following its announcement to acquire Germany-based Nagarro for €1.27 billion. While the deal aims to boost European presence and revenue, brokerages expressed caution over pricey valuations and execution risks.

items.title: Multibagger defence stock Apollo Micro Systems jumps 7% despite stock market sell-off
items.summary: Apollo Micro Systems shares rose over 7% on June 29, following news of a potential ₹30,000-crore order for Bharat Electronics Ltd under the QRSAM program, expected to be approved soon. The company also plans a Board meeting on July 6 for potential fundraising.

items.title: Big boost for Zee Media Corporation! Mauritius-based FII raises stake to 5.04%
items.summary: Zee Media has disclosed that Magnifica Global Opportunities VCC acquired 4.5 crore convertible warrants, raising its holding to 5.04%. The warrants, issued at ₹8.50 each, are convertible within 18 months. Prior to this, the fund held 0.06% of voting shares.

items.title: Omaxe to invest Rs 6,200 crore to expand the hospitality vertical
items.summary: Omaxe is set to invest a substantial Rs 6,200 crore over the next 4-5 years, planning to launch 19 new hotels across five states. These developments, spanning nearly 5 million sq. ft. , will target urban centers, pilgrimage sites, and transit hubs, integrating with Omaxe's existing projects. The expansion aims to bolster recurring revenue and cater to diverse travel needs, with a significant focus on Uttar Pradesh.

items.title: BPCL to buy 40% stake in Tiki Tar and Shell India for Rs 85 crore
items.summary: Bharat Petroleum Corporation is set to acquire a 40% stake in Tiki Tar and Shell India for Rs 85 crore, bolstering its presence in India's expanding value-added bitumen market. This strategic move, approved by DIPAM, aims to capitalize on the nation's infrastructure growth. Tiki Tar and Shell India, a manufacturer and marketer of specialized bitumen products, also exports to neighboring countries.

items.title: Stock market strategy: Buy on dips or sell on rallies? Here’s what experts recommend
items.summary: Stock market strategy: Rather than focusing solely on Nifty 50 stocks, investors should consider companies outside the index that are delivering robust earnings growth. Attractive opportunities currently exist across large-cap, mid-cap and small-cap segments, analyst said.

items.title: HAL announces final dividend of Rs 10 for FY26. Check record date and other details
items.summary: HAL has declared a final dividend of Rs 10 per share (200%) for FY26, subject to shareholder approval at the upcoming AGM. The record date is set for August 14, 2026. The payout will be made within 30 days of approval, along with updates on recent financial performance.

items.title: Alembic Pharmaceuticals gets USFDA nod for generic acne treatment gel
items.summary: Alembic Pharmaceuticals has secured final USFDA approval for its generic Dapsone gel, a topical treatment for acne vulgaris. This approval, for the 5% strength, means the company can now market its version, which is therapeutically equivalent to Almirall's Aczone Gel. This development marks a significant step for Alembic in the US market, offering a new option for acne sufferers.

items.title: Silver futures decline to ₹2.22 lakh/kg
items.summary: On the Multi Commodity Exchange, silver contracts for September delivery fell by ₹746 to ₹2,22,726 per kg with a turnover of 1,988 lots

items.title: NBFC stock Aptus Value Housing Finance climbs over 3%; Citi sees over 30% upside potential
items.summary: Shares of Aptus Value Housing Finance have been enjoying healthy buying interest of late. They have jumped over 35% in the last three months. So far in June, the stock is up more than 3%.

items.title: Aastha Spintex IPO off to a steady start; issue subscribed 52% on Day 1. Check GMP & key details
items.summary: Aastha Spintex IPO saw a steady start on Day 1, subscribed 52% by mid-session, with strong NII demand leading the response. The issue carries a 4% grey market premium, indicating modest listing gains. Proceeds will fund Falcon Yarns acquisition, working capital needs, and general corporate purposes.

items.title: India is plotting a game-changer for RBI, banks, corporates, investors
items.summary: India is planning a high-frequency index to track its vast informal economy, covering millions of unincorporated companies. The proposed MoSPI index could improve GDP estimates, guide RBI interest-rate decisions, strengthen inflation management, sharpen government policymaking and offer investors, businesses and economists an unprecedented real-time view of the country's largest employment engine.

items.title: Some Indian banks poised for interest margin gains as short-term funding costs fall
items.summary: Indian banks are poised for improved interest margins this quarter, thanks to the Reserve Bank of India's liquidity-boosting measures. These initiatives are driving down the cost of short-term funding, with certificate of deposit rates already seeing significant drops. Experts anticipate further easing, suggesting banks can now secure funds more affordably, directly benefiting their profitability and allowing for cheaper refinancing of existing debts.

items.title: South Korea unveils $880bn chip and AI investment plan
items.summary: It comes as regional rivals like Taiwan, China and Japan are investing heavily in chip factories and other technology.

items.title: SEBI invites applications for executive director post
items.summary: The appointment of the executive director will be on a deputation or contractual basis for a period of three years, the Securities and Exchange Board of India

items.title: Oberoi Realty makes Delhi-NCR debut with Rs 6,000 crore housing project
items.summary: Mumbai's Oberoi Realty has officially entered the Delhi-NCR market, launching its maiden luxury housing project, 'Three Sixty North,' in Gurugram. This ambitious venture, with a total investment of Rs 6,000 crore, aims to replicate the company's success in Mumbai. The first phase, featuring 832 units, has commenced with prices starting at Rs 19 crore.

items.title: Organised dairy sector expected to grow by 200-400 bps in FY27: Crisil
items.summary: India's organized dairy sector is poised for accelerated revenue growth of 200-400 basis points this fiscal, driven by consistent volume increases and strategic price adjustments. Despite challenges like El Niño impacting milk supply and raising costs, companies are expected to pass these on, particularly for value-added products. This growth, coupled with healthy cash flows, will support continued investment and stable credit profiles for dairy players.

items.title: Gold futures drop to ₹1,43,380/10g
items.summary: On the Multi Commodity Exchange, August gold contracts fell by ₹782 to ₹1,43,380 per 10 grams with a turnover of 1,592 lots

items.title: 2 top stock recommendations from Vinay Rajani
items.summary: Indian equity markets are experiencing a cautious phase, consolidating within a defined range as fresh buying momentum remains elusive. Technical analysts suggest the broader trend is still positive, with key support at 23,800. Financials and pharma sectors are identified as preferred investment avenues, with specific stock recommendations provided for traders looking for opportunities amidst the current market conditions.

items.title: Nifty IT index crashes 28% YTD. Buy or wait, as renewed US-Iran tension may jeopardise crude oil supply again?
items.summary: Indian IT stocks have declined 28.71% YTD, outpacing the Nifty 50's 8.32% drop due to concerns over slowing global spending. Analysts suggest a tentative recovery, influenced by delayed client decisions and geopolitical tensions, as traditional growth models face disruption from AI.

items.title: West Asia producers push on with oil, LNG loadings despite ship attacks
items.summary: Iran is also accelerating oil loadings after Washington waived sanctions ​on its exports for 60 days

items.title: Protein coffee? Brands cash in on functional beverage boom
items.summary: "We're selling [almost] as much protein cold foam as we do flat whites," Starbuck's EMEA Manager of Beverage Development Sam Henderson told CNBC.

items.title: Oil risks rise, but Moody's sees India holding firm
items.summary: Moody's Ratings believes India can manage a wider fiscal deficit this year without jeopardising its investment-grade credit rating. Despite elevated oil prices posing risks, the agency views their impact as temporary and trusts New Delhi's commitment to fiscal consolidation. While high debt servicing remains a concern, Moody's remains confident in India's steady improvement and conservative path.

items.title: Jivial Industries IPO allotment likely to be out today. Here's step-by-step guide on how to check status
items.summary: The IPO has been priced at ₹196 per share and consists of a fresh issue worth ₹26.65 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of ₹5.34 crore, taking the total issue size to ₹31.99 crore.

items.title: Eleven invests Rs 216 cr into financial services biz, total investment rises to Rs 470 cr
items.summary: Diversified group Eleven has injected Rs 216 crore into its financial services arm, pushing total investment to Rs 470 crore over four years. This strategic move aims to bolster its scaled financial services franchise, with its NBFC, SVCL, having served over 19 lakh customers and disbursed Rs 10,000 crore. Eleven also launched Eleven Tech, a new platform for financial institutions, appointing Monu Ratra as its CEO.

items.title: India's electric car sales set to cross 3 lakh units for first time in 2026: Report
items.summary: India's electric passenger vehicle market is poised for a significant leap, projected to surpass 3 lakh annual sales in 2026. This surge is fueled by a growing array of models, particularly in the affordable segment, coupled with enhanced battery technology that alleviates range anxiety. Attractive ownership costs and rising fuel prices further bolster the appeal of EVs, signaling a strong transition towards mainstream adoption.

items.title: Can Astral's demerger catalyze chemicals growth?
items.summary: Astral's chemicals demerger sharpens business focus, but investors remain unconvinced. The key question is whether the spin-off can revive margins and justify the stock's rich valuation.

items.title: GIFT Nifty hits record open interest of $21.56 billion; contracts touch all-time high
items.summary: GIFT Nifty has hit a record open interest of $21.56 billion, reflecting rising global investor participation in India's offshore derivatives market. The milestone, achieved on June 25, also saw contracts reach an all-time high, reinforcing GIFT Nifty's growing role as a preferred platform for trading Indian equity derivatives.

items.title: Radico Khaitan, Allied Blenders shares get Jefferies’ ‘Buy’ rating; up to 25% upside seen; Should you buy?
items.summary: Jefferies noted that Indian alcoholic beverage stocks have significantly outperformed the broader staples sector during FY23–FY26, delivering an earnings per share (EPS) CAGR of over 30%, compared with mid-to-high single-digit growth for staples.

items.title: Nagarro deal jitters drag Persistent's shares to a two-year low
items.summary: Analysts are concerned about Nagarro's muted growth outlook and intense competition in the enterprise resource planning space.

items.title: Tata Motors CV can cross 1 million vehicles after Iveco deal: N Chandrasekaran at AGM
items.summary: Tata Motors Commercial Vehicles anticipates exceeding one million annual sales post-Iveco acquisition, projecting a global top-four ranking. Despite fuel price concerns, strong infrastructure spending and freight growth are expected to sustain the domestic market. The company is also bolstering non-cyclical revenue streams and expanding its international footprint, aiming for resilient earnings and significant global expansion.

items.title: Rs 8,350 crore crash in Persistent shares explained: Why investors are worried about Nagarro deal
items.summary: Persistent Systems' shares plunged nearly 11% following its announcement to acquire Germany-based Nagarro for €1.27 billion. While the deal aims to boost European presence and revenue, brokerages expressed caution over pricey valuations and execution risks.

items.title: CBSE issues three-language policy guidelines; big update for class 10 students
items.summary: The CBSE has clarified its three-language policy, assuring current Class X students that they are unaffected. Students in Classes VII, VIII, and IX will also be exempt from board exams in a third language upon reaching Class X. Those already studying two foreign languages can continue but must add an Indian language. The board aims for engaging language learning to foster holistic development, with resources to be provided soon.

items.title: Banks, power finance and infrastructure to lead next leg of market rally: Pankaj Pandey
items.summary: India's banking sector is poised for a rally, fueled by robust credit growth, easing deposit pressures, and stable asset quality, according to ICICI Securities' Pankaj Pandey. NBFCs also stand to gain from a softer interest rate environment. While IT acquisitions may take time for significant returns, power financiers and transmission infrastructure present strong long-term investment opportunities. HDFC Bank's governance overhang is lifting, boosting its outlook.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei reverses course to end higher as chip-related shares cut losses
items.summary: Japan's Nikkei index staged a remarkable comeback, closing higher after significant early losses. This reversal was driven by chip-related stocks, which recovered following South Korea's announcement of massive investments in chip and AI mega-projects. Investors shifted focus, boosting companies like Nintendo and Sony, while energy stocks experienced a downturn. The market sentiment reflected a complex interplay of global tech developments and economic concerns.

items.title: The trade-off in the Persistent-Nagarro deal is hard to ignore
items.summary: Persistent Systems' share price fell 11% after the IT company announced the acquisition of Munich-based Nagarro SE – its largest ever – at an enterprise valuation of €1.27 billion.

items.title: Euro zone 10-year yields near three-month lows at start of busy week
items.summary: Bond yields in the Euro zone are at their lowest since early March, a welcome development for central bankers meeting at the ECB's Sintra Forum. A recent drop in oil prices has eased inflation concerns, leading investors to scale back expectations for interest rate hikes from the ECB and Bank of England.

items.title: European shares steady as tech gains offset Middle East ceasefire caution
items.summary: European markets showed resilience on Monday, buoyed by a surge in technology stocks. Investors are cautiously optimistic following a de-escalation of Middle East tensions between the US and Iran. A significant jump in Nagarro's shares boosted the tech sector, which had recently faced a downturn. Analysts are now looking towards central bank commentary for future market direction, with a fragile ceasefire prompting some positive outlooks for European equities.

items.title: Dividend alert! Last day to buy Bajaj Finance, 2 other Bajaj Group stocks for dividends worth Rs 138
items.summary: Investors have a final chance today to buy shares in Bajaj Finance, Bajaj Finserv, and Bajaj Holdings and Investment to be eligible for significant dividends. Bajaj Finance offers Rs 6 per share, Bajaj Finserv Rs 1.50, and Bajaj Holdings and Investment a substantial Rs 130 per share. This opportunity arises due to the T+1 settlement cycle, making today as the last day to secure these payouts.

items.title: Bitcoin slips below $60,000 as ETF outflows and middle east tensions weigh. Here is what experts say
items.summary: Bitcoin dipped below $60,000 as significant ETF outflows and Middle East tensions impacted market sentiment.This marks Bitcoin's weakest June since mid-2022, with over $4 billion withdrawn from spot ETFs this month. Analysts highlight $60,000 as a critical resistance level, while persistent inflation concerns keep investors cautious about riskier assets like cryptocurrencies.

items.title: Advit Jewels IPO allotment to be finalised today. Here's GMP, how to check status
items.summary: Advit Jewels IPO is commanding a grey market premium (GMP) of ₹51 today. The allotment is likely to be finalised on Monday, 29 June.

items.title: Zerodha now wants to enter investment banking space, seeks Sebi nod
items.summary: Zerodha has applied to SEBI for a Category I merchant banking licence, signalling its entry into investment banking. The move aligns with India’s strong IPO pipeline and expanding capital markets activity. If approved, it will deepen Zerodha’s diversification beyond broking into a broader financial services platform

items.title: Fluence deal signals Honasa’s pivot towards health-beauty convergence
items.summary: As beauty consumers increasingly demand science-backed and holistic solutions, Honasa is blurring the lines between cosmetics, wellness and healthcare, with nutraceuticals emerging as a new battleground for growth and differentiation.

items.title: Astral share price slips 10% after demerger announcement. Should you buy or sell?
items.summary: Astral demerger ratio is set at 1:1. This means subject to the necessary approvals, Astral shareholders will receive shares of Astral Chemie in proportion to their existing shareholding (in ratio of 1:1).

items.title: FIIs double stakes in 14 stocks; rally up to 400% in a year, 5 turn multibaggers
items.summary: Foreign Institutional Investors doubled their stakes in 14 stocks within the BSE 1000 universe between March 2025 and March 2026, signalling accumulation. Around 51 stocks saw holdings more than double. Of these, 22 delivered over 25% returns. The highlighted 14 stocks gained 50–400%, with five turning into multibaggers over the year.

items.title: Vinay Rajani of HDFC Sec suggests L&T Finance, Welspun Enterprises shares to buy in near-term
items.summary: Indian equity benchmarks, Nifty 50 and Sensex, declined due to geopolitical tensions impacting investor sentiment. The BSE Sensex fell over 400 pts, and Nifty 50 dropped around 115 pts. Upcoming domestic macroeconomic indicators will guide investors amid concerns over US-Iran military exchanges.

items.title: Sensex drops over 400 points, Nifty slips as geopolitical tensions weigh on markets
items.summary: The weakness was more pronounced in the broader market. The Nifty Midcap 100 index fell 0.82 per cent, while the Nifty Smallcap index declined 0.83 per cent

items.title: Amazon’s rapid delivery push triggers $15-billion rout for Eternal, Swiggy
items.summary: While Eternal has slipped 28% from its October all-time high as of Thursday’s close, Instamart’s owner Swiggy has plunged about 47% from its recent peak in September; that adds up to a selloff of more than $15 billion for the duo

items.title: UK’s Bridgepoint buys real estate unit in $1.4bn bet on US property
items.summary: Private capital group is looking beyond its traditional speciality in corporate buyouts

items.title: South Korea says Samsung and SK Hynix investing in AI, semiconductor mega-projects
items.summary: Shares of Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix declined after South Korea unveiled sweeping artificial intelligence and semiconductor mega-projects.

items.title: IPO GMPs: Advit Jewels IPO, CSM Technologies IPO to Knack Packaging IPO; here's what GMP hints
items.summary: India's primary market remains active with new IPO listings. Turtlemint Fintech debuted at a discount, while Advit Jewels saw strong demand, with a 212.63x subscription. Upcoming IPOs, including CSM Technologies, Aastha Spintex, and Knack Packaging, maintain market momentum despite volatility.

items.title: Small-cap stock Blue Cloud Softech Solution has been empanelled by BSNL. Details here
items.summary: Blue Cloud Softech Solutions share price opened at ₹20.10 apiece today, as compared to previous close of ₹21.03 on Thursday last week.

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank shares drop over 3% as CEO Ashok Vaswani opts out of second term
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank shares witnessed some profit booking as its chief executive officer (CEO), Ashok Vaswani, said he will step down at the end of his current term on 31 December 2026.

items.title: Turtlemint Fintech share price gains 7% after weak listing. Should you buy, sell or hold the stock?
items.summary: Turtlemint Fintech Solutions IPO listing was weaker than the Street estimates, as indicated in the grey market premium (GMP). Ahead of the debut, Turtlemint Fintech IPO GMP today was ₹-5 apiece, signalling a listing with more than 3% discount.

items.title: India seeks EU scrap export relief as curbs threaten trade pact gains
items.summary: India has urged the European Union to reconsider planned restrictions on metal scrap exports. Steel and aluminium producers fear these curbs, set to begin in May 2027, could significantly increase supply shortages and costs. New Delhi is formally seeking continued access to these vital recyclable materials, potentially through export quotas, to safeguard its domestic industries and leverage its recent trade agreement with the EU.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Hostilities halted again as peace talks continue
items.summary: The U.S. and Iran agree to pause fresh hostilities, sending futures higher. But concerns over the peace talks continue, pushing oil back above $70 a barrel.

items.title: ICICI Prudential MF launches multi-asset active fund of funds scheme
items.summary: The scheme will dynamically adjust allocations based on market conditions, valuations and macroeconomic trends

items.title: HDFC Bank shares rise as legal review finds no evidence backing ex-chairman’s allegations
items.summary: The stock rose 1.16% to ₹805.60 in early trade, up from ₹796.30, as investors reacted positively to the review outcome

items.title: Shreedhar Spinners IPO share allotment in focus today: GMP, steps to check status online
items.summary: Shreedhar Spinners' IPO share allotment is expected to be finalised today. Investors can check their status online. Shreedhar Spinners is engaged in the manufacturing of compact spun cotton yarn of various counts. It operates exclusively in the business-to-business (B2B) segment.

items.title: Asian shares mixed as tech stocks fall in Japan and South Korea
items.summary: Tokyo's Nikkei 225 shed 1% to 68,704.70; South Korea's Kospi lost 2% to 8,246.50;

items.summary: Riyaasat Lifestyle IPO allotment date is likely today, 29 June 2026. Investors can check Riyaasat Lifestyle IPO allotment status through the websites of BSE and IPO registrar. Skyline Financial Services is the Riyaasat Lifestyle IPO registrar.

items.title: Anubhav Plast share price hits 5% lower circuit after flat debut
items.summary: Anubhav Plast shares debuted flat at ₹80 on BSE SME, matching the issue price of ₹88. The IPO, with a subscription status of 1.53x, focused on manufacturing ERW steel pipes and aims to expand production capabilities.

items.summary: Turtlemint Fintech IPO listing: Turtlemint Fintech Solutions share price opened at ₹134.90 per share on NSE and 10.39% discount at ₹136 apiece on BSE.

items.title: Baidu shares jump 7% as AI chip arm Kunlunxin said to target $50 billion Hong Kong IPO
items.summary: Baidu's Hong Kong-listed shares rose over 6% amid reports that its AI chip unit Kunlunxin is targeting a $50 billion initial public offering in the city.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty open flat; Dr Reddy's leads gains, Kotak Bank drags

items.title: India regains top spot in EM tracker, but higher cost pressures continue to linger
items.summary: Stronger exports and factory activity helped India reclaim the top spot in Mint's Emerging Markets Tracker. However, higher crude prices during the West Asia war have raised wholesale inflation and input costs, delaying relief for businesses despite the recent easing in oil prices.

items.title: U.S., Iran pause hostilities as Hormuz shipping resumes after weekend clashes
items.summary: The U.S. and Iran agreed to pause hostilities and allow commercial vessels to move freely through the Strait of Hormuz, easing fears of a prolonged disruption to global oil supplies after a weekend of military exchanges.

items.summary: Tokyo’s surging stocks fail to jump-start lacklustre listings market in first half of this year

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Another weekend escalation in Iran, another market shrug
items.summary: Tensions in the Middle East flared again over the weekend, but markets appeared largely unfazed, with investors instead turning to a tech recovery rally.

items.title: From concerts to train rides, bots are winning the ticket wars — but they're only part of the problem
items.summary: From concerts to train reservations, automated bots have become the latest target in the fight against ticket scalping.

items.title: India-Seychelles conclude umbrella line of credit agreement worth Rs 1250 crore
items.summary: India and Seychelles have solidified their partnership with a Rs. 1250 crore line of credit for development projects and an extradition treaty to combat crime. The nations also agreed to integrate India's UPI payment system and explore outer space cooperation. Seychelles requested Indian aid for AI, cybersecurity centers, and a helicopter, which India positively considered. Discussions also touched upon regional security and the Indian Ocean's significance.

items.title: Mindgrove's vision chip moves closer to market with Prama MoU
items.summary: The MoU facilitates joint development efforts, allowing both firms to tailor the product before its commercial launch.

items.title: Inside Rajesh Exports' paper trail—a complex web of auditors and arms
items.summary: A limited-purpose, non-statutory opinion issued by KPMG Switzerland for Global Gold Refineries AG (GGR), the firm's Swiss arm, was likely used to prepare the group's consolidated financial statements, documents reviewed by Mint showed.

items.title: Big Four look beyond metros to tap growth goals of smaller businesses
items.summary: The Big Four consulting firms are expanding into India's tier-2 and -3 cities, drawn by smaller businesses and family-run enterprsies increasingly seeking professionalization and a surge in GCCs. AI platforms and agentic AI are enabling this push, reducing the need for on-ground partner teams.

items.title: Inside the shift in CEO pay structure of a 100-year-old apparel company
items.summary: Raymond Lifestyle has restructured new CEO Satyaki Ghosh's pay package as the company tries to end a run of four CEO exits in five years. The move comes even as the company's revenue and profit have grown post-demerger, while its stock has sharply underperformed peers and the broader market.

items.title: Sovereign funds move from public markets to private to ride AI wave
items.summary: High concentration in stock markets and national security concerns send SWFs to private credit and infrastructure

items.title: AI investment boom may fuel financial risks: BIS
items.summary: The Bank for International Settlements (BIS) has cautioned about the financial dangers linked to the rapid growth of AI, pointing to issues of overinvestment and high valuations. There’s a growing worry that advanced AI models could increase cyber threats, calling for stronger collaborations.

items.title: Plan afoot to create index for unincorporated enterprises
items.summary: The index, proposed by the ministry of statistics and programme implementation (MoSPI), will provide insights into the health of the large yet often difficult-to-measure informal sector.

items.title: Easing of West Asia conflict reduces downside risk to 6.6% growth forecast: Ram Singh, Director, Delhi School of Economics
items.summary: Any moderation in El Nino effects and resolution of geopolitical uncertainty will further reduce the downside risks to growth. Operationalising the FTAs signed with 37 countries will boost exports and FDI, and thus growthRam Singh, Director, Delhi School of Economics

items.title: Seed stocks adequate, but last-mile delivery remains critical: FSII
items.summary: Indian seed companies are holding substantial surplus stock for the upcoming kharif season, aiming to mitigate potential losses from a weak and delayed monsoon due to El Nino. Despite robust production, timely distribution to rain-deficient regions remains a critical challenge. Farmers in vulnerable areas are urged to consider short-duration, climate-resilient seeds as planting windows narrow.

items.title: Aramco helicopter crashes at Saudi port
items.summary: At least 14 killed in Ras Tanura incident just hours after US-Iran strikes threaten fragile deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz

items.title: North-East floods raise power, logistics and inflation risks
items.summary: The disruptions could have wider consequences for the rest of the country, where power demand remains high amid persistent heat and an uneven monsoon.

items.title: China emerges as key growth driver for India's frozen shrimp exports; US remains top market in FY26
items.summary: India's frozen shrimp exports soared to a record high in FY 2025-26, driven by robust demand from China, which emerged as a significant growth market. The United States continues to be the top destination for these exports. This strong performance underscores the growing global appeal of Indian seafood, with the government actively working to diversify export markets and enhance the sector's competitiveness.

items.title: If you think China needs to dethrone U.S. dollar, you don't understand how it is waging global currency war
items.summary: It's misguided to focus on China's renminbi replacing the U.S. dollar. Beijing is already successfully reducing dependence on a dollar-centric global system.

items.title: Raymond’s aerospace vertical growth outpaces precision engineering business
items.summary: Driven by global supply-chain diversification, JK Maini Global Aerospace is clocking a 25% expansion rate and has locked in a ₹2,350-crore order pipeline over the next five years.

items.title: Persistent eyes $5 billion annual revenue by 2031 with mega Nagarro buyout
items.summary: The proposed $1.3-billion deal would double the combined group's European business and add crucial SAP and OpenAI capabilities, propelling the company to become India’s seventh-largest IT exporter.

items.title: NoBroker enters home salon market, stays away from instant help for now
items.summary: NoBroker has launched Zivora in Bengaluru and plans to enter NCR, Pune, Hyderabad, Chennai, and Mumbai in the next two months.

items.title: Modi spotlights shift to public transport, natural fertilizers amid Gulf crisis
items.summary: Addressing the nation in his monthly radio broadcast ‘Mann Ki Baat’, Modi said that following his appeal to avoid foreign travel and cut gold purchases, many people have postponed overseas holidays and chosen not to buy new gold, with some instead recycling old gold to make new jewellery.

items.title: Are AI stocks headed for further turbulence?
items.summary: Market Questions is the FT’s guide to the week ahead

items.title: A 'perfect storm' points to a much smaller U.S. auto market by 2040
items.summary: The auto industry is selling fewer cars--one forecaster says this is a fundamental change, and it is going to get worse.

items.title: India, Seychelles unveil 19 outcomes spanning defence, digital payments, space, health
items.summary: India and Seychelles have inked 19 key agreements, bolstering bilateral ties across defense, maritime security, digital payments, space, and healthcare. Prime Minister Modi and President Herminie finalized pacts including an extradition treaty, UPI integration, and preliminary work on a new hospital. The collaboration signifies a deepening partnership between the two nations, enhancing security and development initiatives.

items.title: Shivraj Singh Chouhan seeks inputs from states to push fresh rural reforms
items.summary: Rural Development Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan urged states to propose new reforms and effectively implement existing schemes to boost villages, crucial for India's developed nation vision by 2047. He highlighted the upcoming Viksit Bharat – Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission-Gramin (VB–G RAM G) replacing MGNREGS, with significant budgetary allocations.

items.title: AI ‘exuberance’ risks ending in lengthy investment bust, BIS warns
items.summary: Weak returns could trigger sharp pullback in funding for tech companies that threatens global economy

items.title: Commerce ministry convenes stakeholders' meet on June 30 on SEZ issues
items.summary: The commerce ministry is convening a stakeholder meeting on June 30 to address critical issues concerning Special Economic Zones (SEZs). Discussions will center on harmonizing export promotion schemes and implementing SEZ reforms, including payment mechanisms for domestic services and job work. A 17-member committee is also working on a comprehensive reform roadmap for a 'SEZ 2.

items.title: As GST turns 10, focus shifts to AI-led compliance, faster refunds, simpler tax processes
items.summary: India's Goods and Services Tax, now in its tenth year, is evolving towards greater efficiency. The government is leveraging AI, data sharing, and simplified processes to cut compliance costs, expedite refunds, and enhance enforcement, especially for MSMEs. This reform has broadened the tax base, boosted compliance, and significantly increased revenues, transforming India's indirect tax landscape into a unified national market.

items.title: Proptech unicorn Square Yards engages bankers for $300-mn IPO
items.summary: The proptech firm is seeking a $2 billion valuation for its planned public listing next fiscal year, following a recent ₹900-crore funding round.

items.title: Spanish import hub chief urges EU to delay ban on Russian gas
items.summary: LNG shipments to the bloc have risen in the wake of the Middle East war

items.title: Mines will hold back Strait of Hormuz shipping for months, CEO warns
items.summary: NYK’s Takaya Soga says safe routes are ‘extremely limited’, restricting traffic to half prewar levels

items.title: Bond giant Pimco flexes muscles with private placements push
items.summary: Firm tries to capitalise on some borrowers’ need to raise cash as boundaries with public markets blur

items.title: Depth over breadth: Piramal Consumer Healthcare takes a leaf out of the modern FMCG playbook
items.summary: The company is cutting new product launches by half and doubling down on quick commerce to chase a $200 million revenue target by 2030, CEO Sai Ramana Ponugoti told Mint.

items.title: Asian Paints is still searching for that perfect finish in home decor
items.summary: In the last four years, Asian Paints' bath fittings, kitchens and lighting business has either declined or stagnated in revenue.

items.title: Aam in Aam, Indians in US savour taste of home
items.summary: Indian mango sales are soaring in the US, hitting record highs this summer. Innovative packaging, targeted promotions, and direct-to-neighborhood deliveries are bringing the beloved 'king of fruits' closer to the diaspora. This surge, driven by nostalgia and a desire for familiar tastes, is even reaching new consumers through major retailers, signaling a significant boost in exports.

items.title: Domestic PCB manufacturing to save Rs 40,000 crore imports annually: Ashwini Vaishnaw
items.summary: India is poised to drastically cut down its dependency on imported Printed Circuit Boards (PCBs), creating an annual savings of around ₹40,000 crore. Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw highlighted that Jewar, Uttar Pradesh, is on track to evolve into a key electronics manufacturing center.

items.title: ₹3,692-crore elevated corridor to redefine Imphal’s skyline
items.summary: Manipur is set to get a massive 25-km elevated corridor in Imphal, a Rs 3,691 crore project aimed at easing traffic and boosting development. The Detailed Project Report has been transferred to NHIDCL, with approval anticipated before the March-April 2027 elections. This ambitious infrastructure initiative promises to transform urban mobility, connect key areas, and signal confidence in the state's future.

items.title: Why investors may want to prioritize bond markets outside the U.S.
items.summary: Allspring Global Investments is pushing clients toward countries with central banks that are raising interest rates or have different inflation dynamics.

items.title: ‘Heated Rivalry’ fuels a boom in gay romance stories, with women leading the fandom
items.summary: ‘Heated Rivalry’ has helped gay romance stories gain mainstream attention, with women driving much of the fandom.
